mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are not significantly positive during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings and generates positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation signals healthy economic growth without pricing pressure, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Hypothesis fails when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% monthly growth yet INDPRO rises, producing negative 20-day returns in over 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening employment conditions that boost household income and corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC remains negative or forward returns are not significantly positive following payroll gains.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth outpaces industrial production indicates demand-pull pressures that trigger monetary tightening expectations and produce negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > INDPRO annualized growth
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 60-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances, invalidating the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 2.0% combined with rising inflation expectations compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced risk appetite.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 60% of cases when both threshold conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate during post-shock recovery phases signals labor market normalization that supports consumer confidence and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in over 50% of recovery-phase unemployment declines, or the signal loses predictive power outside recessionary periods.
